news_summary: Investopedia reports Freeport-McMoRan shares jumped as copper and gold prices rose. Copper futures climbed about 4% after China unveiled stimulus aimed at supporting sluggish housing and financial markets, boosting expected demand. The piece also notes Fed rate cuts weaken the dollar, further supporting copper and gold prices. | Western Metallica announced the start of a first drilling program in October at its Luz Maria copper (Cu-Mo) porphyry prospect within the Caña Brava project in Peru, following permits. The news is an exploration sentiment driver with no immediate supply or demand change; nickel and aluminium are not addressed. | GGL Resources announced Teck’s option agreement for GGL’s Le Champ copper-molybdenum-gold porphyry target in Nevada, including cash option payments and a production-decision milestone. The release supports potential future copper/molybdenum development but provides no near-term output, so any immediate copper price impact is likely limited. | Sputnik reports Russia boosted aluminium shipments to China 1.4x to a record $2.3B in Jan-August, far above 2019’s $60.6M. Total metal supplies to China rose 9%. The increased aluminium supply is likely to pressure near-term aluminium prices, while copper and nickel were more stable.

news_summary: India’s DPIIT mandate requires all aluminium wire rods and rolled products sold domestically to carry BIS certification starting September 26, 2024. Vedanta Aluminium says it is the first and only producer to receive BIS certification for these items. The regulation could limit near-term supply from non-compliant producers. | Carnaby Resources reported results from a VTEM magnetic survey at the Greater Duchess copper-gold JV, identifying multiple new undrilled conductive targets and highlighting the Mohawk corridor with prior 21m at 2% copper. Management expects immediate drilling focus and possible resource expansion, but no near-term output change is provided. | Novelis’ “3x30” initiative targets 75% recycled content and lower CO2 intensity for flat-rolled aluminium by 2030, alongside investments in circularity. It also cites record recycled-content levels, 2.3 million tons recycled in 2023, and plant upgrades to handle contaminated scrap, supporting aluminium demand for low-carbon metal.

news_summary: China’s ETS is set to expand in 2024 to cover aluminium (plus steel and cement), raising covered emissions to 60%. New sectors will initially receive free allowances with no overall cap, while analysts warn the system’s intensity focus and allowance oversupply may limit carbon-price strength, so aluminium price impact appears muted. | Bonnell Aluminum said it is pleased with the U.S. Department of Commerce’s final determinations that impose anti-dumping and countervailing duties on aluminum extrusion imports from 14 countries. The decision is expected to reduce unfairly traded imports and support pricing for aluminum extrusion products.

news_summary: Canada’s Finance Minister said Ottawa could implement a framework to consider requests for tariff relief from a 25% surtax on imported Chinese steel and aluminum due Oct 22. The government is responding to stakeholder concerns about adjustment time, with details to be released later. | Canada will impose a 25% surtax on selected Chinese-made steel and aluminum products. The aluminum component can increase the cost competitiveness of Chinese imports versus domestic supply, tightening near-term availability and supporting aluminium price expectations over coming weeks. | Canada plans to implement a 25% surtax on certain steel and aluminum products from China on Oct. 22, while considering tariff relief where alternative supply is in short demand. The move follows a same-day 100% surtax on Chinese-made electric vehicles. Trade barriers may tighten aluminum availability and support prices. | The report headlines that Canada plans to impose tariffs on Chinese steel and aluminum starting Oct. 22. By restricting Chinese aluminum imports, the measure could reduce near-term availability and shift demand toward alternative suppliers, potentially lifting aluminum price expectations in the coming days. | PA Resources said the US Commerce Department granted it a 0% dumping rate for aluminium extrusion exports after anti-dumping proceedings. The ruling means the products are considered fairly priced and face no extra tariffs in the US, improving competitiveness and potentially supporting aluminium extrusion demand and pricing expectations. | Canada will impose 25% tariffs on a wide range of Chinese steel and aluminum items starting Oct. 22, citing China’s overcapacity and protecting domestic producers. Goods already in transit on the start date are exempt. The policy is framed as aligning with allied trade actions against Chinese industrial supply.

news_summary: The commentary says Jamaica’s Cockpit Country contains Jamaica’s last proven bauxite reserves and describes a new protected-area designation after long Maroon opposition. It argues bauxite/alumina mining harmed health and the environment and questions whether mining areas are included, implying potential supply disruption and downside risk for aluminium. | LME is monitoring tightness after the aluminium October contract premium vs November surged to about $18/ton. One trader holds a large long position in October while most nearby inventory is concentrated in Port Klang. Higher ISTIM re-warrant/cancellation fees could deter delivery, raising prospects of short-covering and sharp price moves near expiry.

news_summary: Commerce issued affirmative final AD/CVD determinations in a U.S. aluminum extrusions investigation, deferring most scope-related comments. Importers face rules around suspension of liquidation and depositing duties (CVD/AD mechanics pending an ITC injury decision within 45 days). The outcome could support domestic aluminum-extrusion supply expectations. | TSR and Novelis agreed a three-year supply deal for 75,000 tonnes of high-quality, pre-sorted end-of-life aluminium scrap for automotive sheet production. The scrap grades TSR130/136 target specific alloys and decarbonization goals. Novelis cites limited scrap availability and the need for long-term supply resilience.

news_summary: Rio Tinto said its Amrun bauxite mine is operating above nameplate capacity, lifting bauxite output, but primary aluminum production fell due to an electricity reduction request at its New Zealand smelter that has ended and ramp-up is underway. The firm also reported lower mined copper output. Overall effects may offset near term. | Australia’s Aluminium Council renewed its call for bauxite, alumina and aluminium to be listed as Critical Minerals, arguing risks from geopolitics, higher costs, and lengthy environmental approvals have impaired and curtailed alumina refining. The group urges faster approvals to secure bauxite feed for refineries and avoid supply disruption. | Supply disruption in Guinea suspended bauxite exports from Guinea Alumina Corporation over customs issues. With Guinea accounting for about 4% of global bauxite supply, alumina prices have surged, creating potential gains for bauxite producers like Metro Mining. The tighter feedstock outlook points to near-term bullish aluminium price pressure. | Rio Tinto reported Q3 copper and aluminium production updates. Copper ramp-up continues at Oyu Tolgoi, while Kennecott faced lower mined output from highwall movement and Escondida improved on higher grades. Aluminium output was slightly lower due to an NZAS electricity-reduction request, now ended and ramp-up underway. | Alumina prices surged to multi-month/all-time highs on SHFE and in spot markets as global supply disruptions and tighter bauxite availability tightened the alumina balance. Rising alumina costs raise input expenses for aluminium smelters (30–40% of costs), potentially squeezing margins and leading to production cuts if aluminium prices lag. | Alcoa says it is nearing a joint funding deal with Spain’s Ignis to support operation of its San Ciprian aluminium plant in Galicia. Alcoa would invest €75m, Ignis €25m for 25% ownership, with potential further funding up to €100m. The plan aims to offset high energy costs and comes amid efforts to sell the plant.

news_summary: Rusal says it will curtail aluminium production by 250,000 tons, citing record-high alumina prices and weaker macro conditions that keep aluminium prices from rising proportionally. Rusal also faces higher costs from buying over a third of required alumina overseas. Demand is shrinking in domestic construction and automotive sectors. | Aldebaran announced an updated mineral resource estimate for its Altar copper-gold project in Argentina, showing a substantial increase versus 2021, including 22.01 billion pounds of copper. news_summary: The EPA denied a Sierra Club petition objecting to the Clean Air Act Title V permit renewal for Warrick Newco LLC, an Alcoa-linked aluminum production plant in Indiana. The petition raised restart/permit compliance concerns for potlines #2 and #6, but EPA said it was a forward-looking compliance issue rather than a current permit-writing problem.

news_summary: ING flags worsening 2025 copper headwinds after Trump’s election, citing potential US tariffs and a stronger dollar that could depress industrial demand. It points to weak China property consumption, high exchange stocks, and a refined surplus. Aluminium is mentioned as relatively advantaged, but macro pressure remains bearish for both. | India’s government is considering an overhaul of an “inverted duty structure” for aluminium ahead of next year’s Union Budget. news_summary: Some Japanese aluminium buyers agreed to pay a premium of $228/mt over the benchmark price for January–March shipments, up 30% from Q4. This fourth consecutive quarterly premium increase is above $175/mt in Oct–Dec, and Japan’s import premiums are a key regional pricing yardstick tied to LME cash. | Japan buyers reportedly agreed to 30% higher aluminium premiums for Q1, according to sources. Higher agreed premiums usually reflect improved demand/availability balance and can translate into stronger near-term aluminium pricing expectations as premiums are closely linked to physical market tightness. | Alumina prices hit record highs in 2024 after production stoppages and disrupted supply routes, with force majeure affecting Australian alumina output and bauxite shipments from Brazil and Guinea. Tight alumina supplies are already causing aluminium curtailments, such as Rusal’s production cut, implying downward pressure on aluminium prices until supply improves. | Ball and Dabur launched Réal Bites juice in fully recyclable aluminium cans in India, highlighting longer shelf life and sustainability versus other packaging. The announcement points to continued growth in India’s juice category and expected can substrate growth, implying incremental aluminum packaging demand, but without large, measurable supply-demand effects for near-term prices.

news_summary: Runaya and Eckart will build a sustainable aluminium powder facility in Orissa to produce spherical atomized aluminium granules and aluminium pigments. It points to growing aluminium powder demand and incremental production capacity. | Bangkok Post highlights Thailand’s Aluminium Loop, a closed-loop system to collect and recycle aluminium cans into new cans. It cites large can manufacturing and plans to expand collection dramatically, supported by recycling and rolling within a tight radius. The initiative aims to boost recycled aluminium supply and reduce costs. | NALCO’s outlook highlights that global aluminium prices have benefited from China’s withdrawal of an export tax rebate, alongside supply disruptions in bauxite. news_summary: China’s primary aluminium net imports fell 23.5% YoY in November to about 132,000 mt, with losses on imports widening above 2,000 yuan/mt after cancellation of aluminium semis export tax rebates from Dec 1. news_summary: Trek’s 2024 sustainability report says most of its emissions come from suppliers’ material processing, with aluminium singled out as a top contributor. Company says 2025 and 2026 output will be impacted, despite bauxite and aluminium-related improvements. | Rio Tinto said Q4 2024 copper production rose 26% year-on-year to 202,000 tonnes, citing ramp-up progress at Mongolia’s Oyu Tolgoi and improved grades at Escondida. It also reported slightly higher bauxite output in Australia, a more indirect support for aluminium feedstock supply. | Port Kembla Copper has sold its former smelter site to developers, enabling rehabilitation of about 300 nearby homes after inspections found historical heavy-metal contamination. The smelter operated from 2000 to closure in 2003, and remediation work will begin post-sale. The news is localized and does not change current metal supply. | METLEN’s board approved a €295.5m project to expand bauxite and alumina capacity and introduce gallium production at Greece’s Agios Nikolaos plant. Production starts 2026-2028, improving EU supply expectations. | The piece headline says Canada’s counter-tariff plan would target US aluminum (and steel). Such trade retaliation can disrupt supply and raise the expected landed cost of US aluminum into Canada, tightening near-term availability and supporting aluminium price sentiment over the next days to weeks.

news_summary: Industry experts urge India to raise import duties on primary and downstream aluminium to 10% and add a 7.5% duty on aluminium scrap to limit cheap imports. They cite rising import dependence and dumping risks; such tariffs could tighten supply and support domestic aluminium prices. | METLEN has approved a €295.5m investment to expand bauxite and alumina production and add gallium capacity at its Aluminium of Greece site in Greece. New bauxite is targeted for 2026, with alumina/gallium ramping from 2027 and full operation by 2028, aiming to reduce Europe’s raw-material import dependence. | Sortera, an Indiana startup, says it can sort thousands of tons of shredded aluminum scrap into specific alloys for high-quality domestic reuse, including automotive and aerospace. By keeping more scrap in the U.S. and enabling lower-energy remelting, it could improve aluminum supply availability and support prices near term.

Alcoa says it secures the Portland smelter’s future and supports competitiveness and jobs. By easing energy-transition burdens and stabilizing supply expectations, the policy is modestly supportive for aluminium prices. | Australia’s government announced a $2 billion green subsidy program for aluminium smelters, intended to keep Rio Tinto’s Bell Bay plant producing and transitioning to renewable electricity. Officials say the plan secures electricity-intensive manufacturing via lower power costs and may stimulate new renewable contracts in Tasmania. | Australia’s federal government will provide a $2 billion green subsidy to aluminium smelters, aimed at keeping smelting jobs and ensuring cheap, reliable electricity for Rio Tinto’s Bell Bay plant as operations shift to renewable energy. It urges policies to cut electricity costs, strengthen CBAM, limit scrap exports, and bolster trade defence and financing for decarbonisation. These steps would likely support near-term aluminium producer outlooks. | Noronex reported multiple strong copper-silver drill intercepts at its Fiesta project in Namibia, including 16m at 1.3% copper equivalent and other wider, higher-grade hits. The company also highlights silver’s potential to improve concentrate payability. Overall, the news is positive for copper sentiment but unlikely to affect near-term market prices materially. | Australia’s federal government proposes tax funding for aluminium producers to generate output using renewable electricity (2028–2036). The article notes smelting is power-intensive and currently coal-based, so reliable low-carbon power could improve competitiveness versus Asia. It frames incentives as supporting green aluminium demand while stabilizing smelter economics.

news_summary: Rio Tinto and Hydro will invest about $45 million over five years to evaluate and develop carbon capture technologies for aluminium electrolysis flue gas. The partnership covers lab work through on-site pilots to speed commercialization. It aims to reduce emissions from existing smelters as part of each company’s net-zero roadmap. | Rio Tinto and Hydro will invest about $45m over five years to assess and accelerate carbon capture technologies for aluminium smelters, targeting reductions in process CO2 emissions from electrolysis. The firms will work with suppliers and also develop carbon-free smelting options, supporting aluminium’s net-zero transition by 2050.

news_summary: Glencore reported 2024 output declines across several commodities, including copper. Copper production fell to 951,600 tonnes, down 6% from 2023 and at the low end of its guidance. Management expects to provide 2025 guidance in February, with improved H2 performance supporting some offset. | China’s Goodsense plans to invest $100 million in Egypt to produce aluminium composite panels in a new Ataqa industrial zone facility. It will partner with WellBond, which already supplies about 8–9% of local needs. The project increases regional aluminium panel supply/demand but signals limited immediate price pressure. | India’s secondary aluminium demand is forecast to rise 7-9% CAGR, with autos contributing an increasing share. Scrap purchasing remains firm, though importers face margin pressure from weak semi-finished demand. Meanwhile, stronger US and Thailand consumption reduces supply to India. Analysts expect aluminium prices to improve from February/March, citing potential China stimulus.

news_summary: The piece frames the energy transition as long-run support for copper, aluminum, and nickel and cites SMM’s January 20, 2025 price levels. news_summary: Group Eleven Resources reports new drill intersections at its Ballywire zinc-lead-silver discovery in Ireland, with copper also present (up to 0.73% Cu in one intercept). However, it’s an exploration update without near-term supply/production implications, so any immediate effect on copper or aluminium prices is likely limited. | Trump’s proposed/partially delayed tariffs raise duties on Canadian/Mexican and Chinese aluminium imports, and the U.S. is a net importer with heavy dependence on Canada. Enforcement would redirect Canadian volumes to Europe, leaving a U.S. supply gap and pushing the Midwest premium higher, increasing downstream costs. | Tajikistan’s minister says primary aluminium output rose 24% in 2024 to 83,000 tonnes, with growth continuing into 2025 after planned reduction-cell overhauls. The minister also cites record copper production and talks with a Chinese firm to build a copper rod plant, but provides limited near-term export detail.

news_summary: Aluminium Deutschland urges Germany to improve competitiveness as production fell for the third year in a row. 2024 output declined 2% to about 2.7 million tonnes, with semi-finished output down 3% and extrusions down 7%. It presses for cheaper power and hydrogen, scrap security, and trade protection.

Australian smelters and manufacturers fear margin pressure and risk of cheaper Chinese aluminium being redirected and potentially dumped. Tariff timing is March 12, with impacts uncertain but near-term uncertainty high. | Trump’s 25% tariff on imported aluminium is expected to raise aluminium packaging costs for Coca-Cola. Management says it could increase use of PET bottles if aluminium cans get too expensive, although it downplays the overall cost impact. The change implies weaker near-term demand for aluminium packaging. | Alliance Nickel’s NiWest project in Western Australia has published a definitive feasibility study and claims low-cost, low-carbon nickel sulphate production with a ~10% premium over LME nickel. Despite a depressed nickel market tied to Indonesian supply, FID is not until ~Aug 2025, limiting near-term price impact. | EU envoys approved a 16th sanctions package targeting Russia, including a phased ban on primary aluminum imports into the EU. The measures also expand restrictions on circumvention through Russia’s shadow fleet and add more entities and individuals. The aluminum import ban is expected to tighten supply and support aluminum prices. | EU envoys agreed a 16th sanctions package on Russia that includes a ban on imports of primary Russian aluminium from a future effective date, with a limited 275,000 tonnes phase-in. news_summary: Workers at Australia’s Tomago aluminium smelter (Rio Tinto) began an 8-hour strike stoppage on Sunday. The walkout adds to existing stress in the aluminium industry as the US considers potential tariffs. Reduced smelter output can tighten near-term supply expectations and pressure aluminium prices. | Hindalco signed an MoU with Madhya Pradesh to invest Rs 15,000 crore over two years, expanding its aluminium presence in the state. Management also cited US tariffs as neutral-to-positive for the group’s consolidated position. | Hindalco signed an MoU with Madhya Pradesh to invest Rs 15,000 crore over two years to expand aluminium smelting capacity. The announcement increases expectations of higher aluminium production and supply growth, which could pressure aluminium prices over the next several weeks as traders price in future output. | Reuters reports that one party holds up to 90% of available LME aluminium inventories, with 32,175 tonnes cancelled in a week. Concentration and cancellations reduce near-term deliverable supply. This comes amid aluminium tightness and strong backwardation after the EU banned Russian primary aluminium imports. | Trump’s administration plans to impose 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum exports from Canada starting March 12. The lower-emissions metal is intended for lightweight auto components and to help meet carbon-neutral goals and EU CBAM requirements. Reuters says the policy could raise costs for beer imports worth $7.5 billion in 2024 and influence short-term aluminum-related demand via can supply chains. | Chicago apartment developers may delay new projects because newly imposed tariffs on steel and aluminum raise costs amid high interest rates and labor expenses. The article links these tariff-driven cost pressures to slower construction starts, which can weaken near-term demand outlooks for aluminum used in building materials. | Novelis’ aluminum plant in Chesterfield is set to close on May 30. The stoppage is likely to reduce near-term output and availability tied to aluminium supply chains, potentially tightening supply conditions for aluminium products and supporting prices over the next days to a few weeks. | The article says China’s MIIT plan aims to green and restructure aluminum production but comes as China nears its capacity ceiling and production growth is expected to slow. news_summary: Norsk Hydro reported Q1 profit growth but slightly missed forecasts and cut its 2025 outlook for its aluminium extrusions segment due to weaker downstream demand and rising uncertainty. The article notes headwinds in recycling margins and aluminium trade disruptions from renewed U.S. tariffs that have roiled flows and premiums, adding cost pressure. | Norsk Hydro reported higher core profit but cut its 2025 extrusion outlook on weaker downstream demand and compressed recycling margins. The return of U.S. 25% aluminium tariffs has roiled trade flows and pushed physical premiums to record highs, raising import costs and tightening regional pricing. Chinese surplus and weaker demand temper. | Hydro signed a long-term, ~1 billion euro deal to supply low-carbon aluminium wire rod to NKT for power cable production supporting Europe’s grid upgrades. The contract runs 2026–2033 (option for more) and follows Hydro’s planned Karmøy smelter expansion, reinforcing aluminium demand and supply expectations; aluminium can substitute copper in grids. | Norsk Hydro reported sharply higher core profit on higher aluminium prices and currency gains, while cutting its extrusion outlook due to weaker downstream demand. Renewed U.S. 25% aluminium tariffs roiled trade flows, pushing physical market premiums to record highs and increasing costs for U.S. buyers, supporting near-term aluminium tightness. | U.S. aluminum import costs have surged after new tariffs (including 20% from the EU and 10% from the UK) took effect, driving Midwest delivery premiums for physical shipments toward record highs. Supply chain disruptions, shipping delays, and higher freight/risk premiums are pushing domestic aluminum prices near multi-year peaks.

news_summary: NKT signed a roughly €1bn agreement with Hydro to secure low-carbon aluminium wire rod supply through 2033, with first rights to Hydro’s REDUXA grades. Hydro is investing €140m in a new casthouse to expand output locally. The deal supports aluminium demand and supply expectations, especially for European low-carbon material. | U.S. aluminium tariffs are lifting U.S. aluminium premiums while depressing European premiums, as metal imports are diverted abroad and U.S. demand remains constrained by limited primary output. With duties and logistics restricting arbitrage, U.S. buyers face higher costs likely to persist for months, barring policy relief.

news_summary: Alcoa’s CEO Bill Oplinger says the Midwest aluminium premium is set to soar, with no plan to shut the Portland aluminium smelter for the next decade. news_summary: Indonesia’s bauxite association (ABI) says many Chinese smelters refuse to pay the government-set benchmark for bauxite ore, forcing several miners to stop producing. With Indonesia banning bauxite exports to feed domestic alumina/aluminium, weaker miner output could tighten bauxite supply for smelters and support aluminium prices. | SMM’s AICE 2025 forum covers how new alumina production in Indonesia could affect China’s alumina market, alongside a 2024-2025 review. It highlights alumina price declines on surplus fundamentals and expects 2025 to remain oversupplied, implying lower feedstock costs for smelters and supporting aluminium profitability. | Spain has seen a sharp rise in railway copper cable thefts, including on the Madrid–Seville high-speed line, disrupting sensors and forcing trains into safety mode. Theft is driven by scrap economics and reported flows to global buyers. Preventive measures may include replacing some copper with aluminium or fibre optics. | SMM reports China’s imported bauxite port inventories rose sharply by 4.13 million mt in April to 20.19 million mt, amid high import supply and declining demand from alumina refineries undergoing maintenance/production cuts. Refiners also shift toward cheaper domestic bauxite, reducing import purchases and sustaining bauxite price pressure. | The US imposed additional aluminium tariffs on April 9, lifting effective EU duties to 45% (plus the existing 25% Section 232 base). The Midwest Premium surged to above 33 cents/lb, near 2018-2019 highs. Middle East-driven shipping reroutes and port constraints further raise logistics costs for US aluminium. | Israel will apply anti-dumping tariffs of up to 146% on Chinese aluminum imports after launching an investigation into allegedly underpriced shipments. The government moves to impose levies before the investigation ends, aiming to protect local producers from “flooding” by 25 Chinese companies. | Brixton Metals announced 2025 exploration plans at its Thorn copper-gold project, including shallow drilling for gold mineralization with reported copper values and planned drilling of new copper porphyry targets Catalyst and Tempest. The news is exploration-focused with no production or supply disruption, so near-term copper impact is likely limited. | SMM reports China’s alumina prices dropped significantly in April, pulling down the average tax-inclusive full cost of producing aluminium by 3.7% MoM. Alumina input costs fell ~10.4% MoM and auxiliary costs are expected to decline in May, supporting lower aluminium production costs and margins going into May. | The U.S. raised aluminum import tariffs to 25% (effective March 12, 2025) and ended exemptions, aiming to restore domestic capacity. Canada retaliated with tariffs on U.S. aluminum products. The article argues U.S. smelters are idled due to high power costs, making a quick capacity rebound unlikely, implying tighter supply and supportive prices for aluminum. | SMM reports that domestic aluminium ingot inventories fell into an absolute multi-year low pre-Labour Day, then saw only a limited first buildup after the holiday. Lower-than-expected ingot arrivals kept buildup manageable and said no significant short-term price pressure occurred, while billet inventories face some risk if outflows weaken.

news_summary: Weekly review shows secondary aluminum alloy prices (ADC12) easing while demand remains weak as holiday-related inventory stocking is limited. Social inventory of secondary ingots rose sharply and shipping pressure increased, but aluminum scrap supply is tight and cost support persists. Outlook is rangebound with upside capped by orders. | SMM cites customs data showing April 2025 net primary aluminium imports rose to about 236,800 mt, with net imports at a new high, and expects May inflows to remain elevated. It attributes this to loss-making overseas supply, weak demand, destocking, and increased processors’ willingness to take manual processing orders.

The WA EPA is taking public submissions on multiple mine proposals. If regulatory outcomes delay capacity, aluminium supply expectations could worsen. | SMM data show May 2025 aluminum casting ingot production fell 6.15% YoY to about 913,000 mt as smelters in north China increased the share of liquid aluminum to 75.5% (+1.48 pp MoM). It cites 350,000 tonnes of UAE aluminium exports to the US in 2024 and notes potential trade talks and Dubai’s transshipment capacity, implying limited immediate downside for aluminium pricing. | The U.S. Department of Commerce’s BIS is seeking public comment on adding more construction-related materials into the scope of existing 25% Section 232 steel and aluminum tariffs. If inclusions are approved, additional aluminum imports could face higher costs, affecting construction demand and input procurement. Comments are due June 4, 2025. | SMM reports China’s bauxite output rose in May and inventories increased at alumina refineries and nine ports, showing a continuing surplus. While refineries’ operating capacity may rebound in June and demand could rise, imported bauxite supply remains high, so bauxite inventories are expected to keep building in the near term.

news_summary: Trump’s planned/implemented higher US tariffs on steel and aluminium are squeezing Indian metal exporters and foundries. Orders have slowed and nearly 30% were cancelled after tariffs rose to 50%. With a 26% reciprocal tariff on India in play and market uncertainty, aluminium-linked supply and demand weaken, bearish for prices. | India announced incentives to attract foreign firms to build copper smelters and refineries, aiming to cut refined copper import dependence. The plan includes subsidies and customs duty exemptions, concentrate procurement strategy via trade talks, and notes tighter concentrate supplies. It also outlines aluminium demand and production strategies, but mainly longer-term targets.

news_summary: Emirates Global Aluminium started production at an expanded Spectro Alloys aluminium recycling facility in Minnesota, adding 55,000 tonnes of secondary billet capacity in the first phase. It will expand further with 165,000 tonnes annually once fully ramped. EGA also plans more recycling and a new US primary smelter. | SMM data shows China’s aluminum wire and cable industry operating rate fell to 52.01% in June, down 3.4% MoM and 4.65% YoY. Inventories declined on slower terminal pickup and high aluminum prices. New State Grid tenders slowed, suggesting continued weaker order acceleration in July despite longer-term power investment. | SMM reports June aluminum rod production and operating rates fell, while in-plant inventories rose due to weak downstream purchasing and off-season demand. news_summary: Alcoa says Trump’s Section 232 aluminium tariffs doubled to 50% in June, prompting supply-chain reengineering and diverting Canadian-origin metal away from the US. The Midwest US P1020A premium has surged over 200% this year, implying tighter US availability. The firm expects the premium to rise further as the US market shortens. | BHP reported record copper output across its operations, including Chile’s Escondida at the highest level in 17 years, and provided FY2026 guidance of 1.8–2.0 million tonnes. It also updated iron ore and capex, while citing resilient demand supported by renewables and EV investment. No aluminium-specific signals were provided. | Fastmarkets International’s aluminium 2025 event promotion emphasizes contract-season pricing and supply-demand dynamics. news_summary: Trek will source nearly all aluminum bicycle frames from facilities powered by renewable energy and/or using recycled content, making low-emission aluminium the default across its alloy lineup from October 2025. news_summary: A Port of Oswego FTZ allows aluminum importers to temporarily cushion the impact of Trump’s 50% tariff on Canadian raw aluminum. Companies previously stockpiled ahead of tariff increases and use bonded handling and storage solutions. Importers order less due to logistical limits, but FTZ relief helps sustain supply access.

news_summary: The article argues a hypothetical 50% tariff on copper imports would raise domestic copper costs, fragment trade, and potentially reduce global mining activity. It expects copper-intensive demand to soften and price divergence between domestic and LME/SHFE benchmarks to emerge. It also mentions limited substitution into metals like aluminium but focuses mainly on copper. | Cameroon signed financing for Proalu SA’s Douala aluminium transformation plant, backed by local banks and Proparco. The project will produce 30,000 tons of aluminium-related products and electrical cables, including 2,000 tons aluminium and 6,000 tons copper, sourcing raw materials from Alucam. It supports import substitution and state revenue.

Despite improved supply circulation and some inventory build, pre-holiday stocking and rigid costs provide support, implying high short-term volatility. | NexMetals reported assay results from 2 of 12 metallurgical drill holes at the Selkirk Cu-Ni-Co-PGE mine in Botswana. news_summary: Aluminum prices hit a 6-month high as LME breaks out on supply deficit fears that China’s output is nearing its production cap. Despite soft Chinese domestic demand and rising production data, inventories remain low and Russian material supply is constrained by sanctions, supporting a near-term bullish tone.

news_summary: Copper rose 0.1% on Tuesday as supply tensions (Grasberg production suspension in Indonesia) outweighed stock accumulation. US Comex reserves jumped on tariff expectations, keeping the outlook fragile. A Fed rate cut improved sentiment. Nickel gained on Indonesia permit suspensions, while aluminium eased slightly.

news_summary: Canada’s trade minister said Ottawa hopes to negotiate reductions or elimination of U.S. sectoral tariffs, including a 50% tariff on Canadian steel and aluminum. He cited potential pressure from U.S. manufacturers using Canadian aluminum and suggested talks could progress before the USMCA review, supporting aluminum demand expectations.

news_summary: Cameroon’s raw aluminium exports fell 27.7% in Q1 2025 to 8,000 tons, with revenue dropping to CFA11 billion from CFA14 billion a year earlier. The decline reflects Alucam’s ongoing losses after Rio Tinto’s withdrawal. A new processing plant is planned, but not operating until 2027. | Brazilian Rare Earths released a maiden resource estimate for its Amargosa bauxite-gallium project, describing large-scale, low-silica bauxite suitable for direct shipping and improved alumina refining. news_summary: Canada’s government removed tariffs on certain U.S. and Chinese steel and aluminum imports, with the Finance Ministry order dated Oct. 15/confirmed Oct. 17. The move is intended to ease pressure on Canadian industry and follows prior tariff actions. Analysts may view it as supportive for near-term aluminum sentiment. | Angola plans to launch large-scale aluminium production in December 2025 at a new facility in the Barra do Dande Free Zone. The $1.6 billion, China-linked project aims to produce aluminium ingots, alloys, and sheets for export, boosting domestic capacity and employment as the country diversifies away from oil. | Electricity failures at Nordural’s Icelandic aluminium smelter cut output by two-thirds, including a full potline shutdown, while Mozambique’s Mozal faces power-supply stalemate that could limit production. With Europe’s duty-free ‘green’ P1020 supply tight into 2026, premiums rise and traders warn of a potential deficit. | Weekly review reports a slight rise in primary A00 aluminium and a stronger ADC12 spot market, driven mainly by tight aluminium scrap supply. Scrap procurement prices increased, spreads between primary and scrap widened, and destocking reduced secondary alloy ingot inventory. Costs remain rigid despite minor pullbacks in silicon/copper, supporting aluminium bids. | Fastmarkets issued upward corrections to the FOB Brazil inferred alumina index (MB-ALU-0010) in October 2025, citing procedural methodology lapses. The revised higher alumina price boosts producer revenues but raises immediate raw-material costs for aluminum smelters, likely pressuring aluminum margins and near-term aluminum sentiment. | Alcoa says U.S. tariffs on Canadian aluminum cost it an extra US$69 million versus the prior quarter, with further increases expected. However, rapidly rising U.S. aluminum prices—driven by declining inventories and imports and a wider Midwest premium—have largely offset the tariff hit, leaving overall pressure mixed. | A fire at Novelis’ aluminum plant in Oswego halted production and prompted Ford to work with Novelis to source substitute aluminum for its still-operational cold-rolling section. Ford estimates up to $1 billion in earnings impact and other automakers also rely on Novelis, implying near-term aluminum supply tightness.

news_summary: Norsk Hydro, a major shareholder in Australia’s Tomago aluminium smelter, marked the asset down to zero and said operations are increasingly challenging due to difficulty finding affordable renewable electricity. The smelter is a major electricity consumer, so higher power costs and potential uncertainty around output raise short-term concerns for aluminium supply. | Delemar Industrial Group announced an EGP 5 billion expansion in Egypt, adding multiple production lines for aluminium profiles and processing capacity, including anodizing and profile paint plants. The plant is being connected to the national grid and a substation is 90% complete, supported by commercially viable gas supply agreements, which could boost output and supply local industries. | Canada’s Quebec “aluminum valley” is facing 50% U.S. tariffs that scramble integrated supply chains and cut competitiveness for Canadian aluminum products, with the sector claiming major revenue losses. The report highlights a stronger US dollar as a macro headwind for dollar-denominated commodities, noting copper typically falls with USD strength, pressuring expected revenues and valuation sentiment. | Tomago Aluminium has started employee consultations after failing to find commercially viable energy pricing for operations beyond 2028. Electricity costs are over 40% of its costs, and proposed coal- and renewable-power options for January 2029 would raise costs sharply. A final closure decision is pending, but consultation runs into November. | Tomago Aluminium has started employee consultations after failing to secure commercially viable electricity supply options after its current contract expires. Power costs—over 40% of operating costs—are projected to rise sharply for both coal and renewables from January 2029, potentially ending smelting operations. | Rio Tinto said Australia’s largest aluminium smelter Tomago may be forced to cease operations because it cannot source commercially viable electricity rates after its power deal expires in December 2028. With power over 40% of operating costs, high energy prices threaten output, prompting consultation on potential shutdown. | Tomago Aluminium has started consultations with employees about potentially ceasing operations by 2028 after failing to secure competitively priced energy for post-contract supply. The company blames high electricity prices and delays in renewable supply for rising costs. news_summary: The piece argues Tomago Aluminium is crucial for NSW grid stability and industrial load, highlighting its large electricity use and new transmission plus grid-scale battery projects enabling more renewable power to reach the smelter. It warns that a hypothetical shutdown would undermine energy reliability and Australia’s aluminium manufacturing sovereignty. | A Camden Haven article highlights Tomago Aluminium’s role in maintaining NSW grid stability and supplying a large, modifiable industrial load while coal plants retire. It stresses that transmission links and new batteries help ensure continuous smelter operations. It warns that any Tomago closure would increase reliance on imports. | The article highlights that the Tomago aluminium smelter in NSW faces a potential closure linked to high energy prices. It argues this challenges the government’s renewables narrative about lower costs. The implied impact is higher operating costs and possible reduced aluminium supply, which would weigh on aluminium prices. | The article says Tomago Aluminium near Newcastle uses about 12% of NSW electricity and is critical for grid stability as coal retires. It highlights new transmission and battery projects that improve renewable integration and argues Tomago’s continued operation prevents higher costs and import dependence. | US aluminium prices face upward pressure from higher costs due to Section 232 tariffs on primary aluminium (including a 50% P1020 import tariff) and a supply shock after a Novelis rolling mill outage reduced automotive-grade sheet. Argus launched new scrap assessments as spreads widen and availability tightens for automotive alloy supply. | Hunter businesses worry Tomago Aluminum may become commercially unviable, after a consultation process found no sustainable operating pathway beyond 2028. Proposed cost increases for coal-fired and renewable options from January 2029 raise closure risk, threatening export metal shipments and local jobs, creating potential aluminium supply concerns. | Local businesses warn that Tomago Aluminum may become commercially unviable beyond 2028, with costs for coal-fired or renewable options under market proposals expected to rise sharply from January 2029. Possible closure would disrupt jobs and export shipments, implying reduced aluminium supply. | Hunter businesses fear Tomago Aluminium may not remain viable beyond 2028, with costs of coal-fired or renewable options rising significantly from January 2029. A potential closure would impact freight and regional manufacturers. The smelter is described as important for grid stability, and no final decision has been made.

news_summary: SMM reports alumina production costs are set to fall further in November as bauxite costs weaken and caustic soda contract prices decline across regions. Weighted average full alumina cost should drop slightly to 2,900–3,000 yuan/ton, with cash cost at 2,710–2,810 yuan/ton. Alumina margin pressure eases. | India’s Aluminium Association urged the government to impose a flat 15% customs duty on aluminium products to counter a more than 50% rise in imports from surplus countries. It argues the measure would prevent dumping of poor-quality scrap, protect domestic manufacturing, and encourage investment, potentially supporting aluminium prices. | SMM data show China’s aluminium industry costs fell in October as alumina prices declined, lifting average tax-inclusive profits to about 5,022 yuan/mt. Operating capacity was fully profitable. Alumina surplus and open import windows support lower costs, though anodes and fluoride salts diverge. November costs are forecast slightly lower. | Glencore plans to close Canada’s Horne smelter, citing high costs for environmental upgrades, with no closure date yet. Horne is Canada’s largest copper smelter and is estimated to produce over 300,000 tonnes annually. The shutdown would intensify expectations of refined copper shortages amid existing supply disruptions. | Alcoa’s shares rise as LME aluminium hits a 17-month high near $2,900/t. The move is attributed to tighter global supply from China’s production/environmental caps, outages, lower inventories, and a surge in green-energy-linked demand (EVs, solar, grids), supporting further bullish near-term aluminium pricing despite higher downstream costs. | Guinea’s bauxite exports rose to 48.6 million tons in Q1 2025 (+39% y/y) as Chinese aluminum production recovered and port capacity expanded from five to nine export-ready facilities. Government shipping mandates and refinery-related initiatives support continued growth, implying stronger bauxite availability for aluminum markets in 2026. | Guinea exported 48.6 million tons of bauxite in Q1 2025, up 39% year-on-year, driven by stronger Chinese demand and expanded port infrastructure. The article connects the boom to rising Chinese aluminium output and expects continued growth in 2026 supported by logistics upgrades and government policy on value-chain management. | India’s Aluminium Association of India has asked for a flat 15% basic customs duty on imported aluminium products to counter a 50%+ rise in imports over five years and concerns about poor-quality scrap inflows. The proposal aims to protect domestic smelting, attract investment, and reduce dumping pressures, which could be price-supportive.

news_summary: A fire at Novelis’ Oswego, New York aluminum plant shut down the hot mill, suspended shipments of finished aluminum sheet, and disrupted supply to Ford and other automakers. The facility makes about 40% of US automotive-grade specialized aluminum sheet, idling Ford plants and risking revenue while recovery delays persist. | A federal jury awarded JW Aluminum $112 million after insurers denied coverage for a 2020 fire at its Goose Creek aluminum mill. Jurors found insurers breached an all-risk policy, including $32.3 million for repairs and $80 million for business-interruption losses, with possible appeal expected.

news_summary: Secondary aluminum alloy and aluminum scrap prices stabilized at high levels this week, tracking primary aluminum. Tight scrap supply (low yard inventories, difficult recycling, and a sharp import drop) plus steady casting demand and year-end auto order front-loading supported prices. Import windows remained costly and constrained, keeping sentiment firm and volatility elevated.

The changes support future CelestiAL/MinimAL low-carbon output, but ramps begin from Q4 2025. | The article explains how the U.S. expanded Section 232 coverage through an inclusion process for steel and aluminum derivative products, starting in April/May 2025. Disruptions, low ore grades, concentrate shortages and smelter output cuts (due to weak TCRCs) are tightening physical markets, keeping prices elevated.

news_summary: Copper fell from an all-time peak on the LME, down 0.8% as a stronger dollar and risk-off caution weighed on dollar-priced commodities. Other base metals also declined, including aluminium and nickel. The retreat is viewed as temporary, with supply-squeeze worries and copper support above $11,000. | The piece describes frequent changes to US tariffs and trade deals with short notice, including detailed duties on steel and aluminum derivatives. It explains how customs brokers and CBP must rapidly update classifications and enforcement. These measures can increase landed costs for aluminium imports in the near term. | Copper surged to record highs as supply fears intensified: LME inventories fell sharply, warehouse withdrawals increased, and cash premiums widened alongside mine disruptions, lower output guidance, and coordinated Chinese smelter reductions. It also cites potential aluminium substitution in some power applications, but the dominant driver is tight copper physical supply. | Recycled aluminum demand is rising as energy costs keep primary smelting expensive and EU/automotive policies push higher recycled content. Producers including Novelis and Hydro are expanding recycling capacity, while China’s primary smelting cap increases reliance on scrap. These factors support aluminium market sentiment and likely near-term price strength.

Aluminum prices surged above 22,000 yuan/mt, and environmental restrictions in Henan re-emerged, constraining production and deliveries. | SMM reports aluminum fluoride prices jumped 250–300 yuan/mt in early December after downstream aluminium enterprise tender prices were finalized at 10,200–10,300 yuan/mt. Demand remains mediocre, keeping procurement weak. | Argus hosted a webinar on its newly launched US aluminium scrap price assessments for key auto scrap grades (baled clips). news_summary: Mexico’s Chamber of Deputies and Senate approved reforms to its General Import and Export Tax Law that change aluminium tariff schedules. Duties on some primary aluminium HS codes are removed, while new levies are added for various aluminium bars, wires and structures (often around 35%). Implementation is set for 2026, affecting trade flows.

news_summary: AFASL warns a policy gap in Sri Lanka’s BOI Negative List allows duty-free imports of fully fabricated aluminium doors, windows and façade systems, while duty protection applies mainly to raw extrusions. The resulting import undercutting is said to threaten local jobs and reduce domestic value-chain demand, pressuring expectations for aluminium. | NALCO expects India’s aluminium demand to stay strong as upcoming budget thrusts on infrastructure and power sector expansion boost consumption of conductors, cables and construction-linked uses. The firm is scaling up alumina output and is ahead of targets, with steady aluminium production plans despite subdued prices. | NALCO’s CMD said India’s aluminium demand should stay strong as the government is expected to keep prioritising infrastructure development and power sector expansion in the upcoming Union Budget. The statement implies improved demand outlook for aluminium, which could support short-term aluminium price expectations. | The article weighs scenarios for Indonesia’s 2026 bauxite RKAB quotas (18, 22, or 25 Mt) against 23–25 Mt projected demand tied to alumina refinery expansions. A most-likely 18–22 Mt outcome implies tighter supply discipline and potential bauxite price normalization, supporting higher near-term expectations for aluminium-related feedstock costs.

news_summary: China’s export tax rebate adjustment (ending the 13% rebate for aluminum semis and copper semis as of Dec 1, 2024) is linked to a fall in 2025 aluminum semi exports and a shift toward processing trade with imported materials. SMM expects higher processing trade share in 2026, supporting export competitiveness. | Rio Tinto said 2025 copper equivalent output rose 8% and copper production climbed 11% YoY, with shipments up 5%, helped by Oyu Tolgoi ramp-up. The company also reported increased aluminium output (up 3% for the year) alongside bauxite growth, implying improved supply momentum. | China’s customs data show unwrought aluminum alloy imports fell in 2025 but December imports rose MoM, while exports increased YoY. The weak import trend was tied to inverted domestic/overseas price spreads and higher overseas alloy ingot prices. Late-Dec/early-Jan ADC12 prices rose, reopening the import window and supporting aluminium demand expectations. | NITI Aayog released a decarbonisation roadmap for aluminium (and cement/MSMEs), projecting a major rise in aluminium output and highlighting high electricity-driven emissions. It recommends renewable power, dedicated RE grids, and transmission utility reforms for near-term action, aiming to reduce emissions and manage potential future export restrictions.

news_summary: SMM expects China’s alumina capacity to rise to 118.92 million mt in 2026, with 8.6 million mt of new projects starting and 7.6 million mt in Guangxi. The added supply shifts Guangxi toward surplus, likely driving regional and national alumina price declines and increasing global competition, bearish for aluminium. | South32 reported 3% higher December half-year alumina output and a 2% rise in aluminium production, citing record Brazil Alumina performance and increased Hillside capacity testing. Mozal ceased pot relining ahead of a planned March 2026 care-and-maintenance shift. Copper production met expectations with strong overall first-half volumes. | South32 reported a 3% rise in alumina production and 2% higher aluminium output in the December 2025 half-year, citing record Brazil Alumina performance and Hillside capacity testing, plus Mozal pot relining ahead of planned care and maintenance. Copper production was in line with expectations, so near-term effects are likely small. | Abitibi Metals announced additional high-grade copper-gold intercepts from Phase 3 drilling at the B26 polymetallic deposit in Québec, including 12.83% CuEq over 5.0 metres. The company is expanding drilling into Phase 4 and highlights resource growth potential, implying incremental bullish sentiment for copper, but no confirmed near-term supply change.

news_summary: SMM cites China’s aluminum wire and cable exports rising 42.9% YoY in 2025, with December also higher YoY. Growth is linked to renewed overseas spending on renewable energy and power grid modernization, supported by specific national policies in Australia and Tanzania. Export momentum is expected to persist into 2026. | Prospect Ridge reports final IP survey results identifying a large chargeability/resistivity/magnetic target at its Excalibur copper-gold porphyry project and expects to finalize drill targets and begin a maiden diamond program in June 2026, subject to permits. The update is sentiment-supportive for copper but no near-term supply effect is stated.

news_summary: Kibar Americas purchased the former Novelis plant site in Fairmont, West Virginia, after the facility closed in March 2025. The company will assess options for the site’s future use and coordinate with utility partners to align energy supply with redevelopment plans, potentially supporting aluminum-related processing activity. | Reuters data referenced in the article indicate nickel prices increased as markets bet Indonesia may curb output growth, amid uncertainty over quotas and supply tightness. It also reports aluminium rising on supply concerns following Iranian strikes on major Middle Eastern aluminium facilities, alongside ongoing nickel and base-metal volatility. | Spot copper treatment charges in China have fallen to historic lows (about -70 USD/dmt), threatening smelter profitability. Smelters are still running high output as surging sulfuric acid prices offset TC losses. Geopolitical risks around Hormuz/Red Sea may keep acid prices high, supporting copper supply and near-term price tone. | Drone and missile attacks on Iranian-linked infrastructure have damaged aluminium smelting facilities in the Gulf, with EGA halting operations at its Al Taweelah smelter after power-plant damage and Alba expected to run at ~30% utilisation. Analysts flag a major supply crisis and aluminium prices have jumped sharply. | The piece says Hormuz and regional conflict uncertainty has driven higher energy costs, increasing aluminium smelting expenses and supporting a cost floor. Strait of Hormuz shipping disruptions linked to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran have constrained supplies to Gulf smelters, which have cut operations and reduced alumina demand.
